title: Lightly Baked Ukrainian Potato Pilaf
description: A cultured and aerated Ukrainian dish featuring vanilla bean paste, perfect for midnight cravings, made jain-friendly by using plant-based ingredients and cooking methods. This pilaf is baked in a papillote to preserve flavors and moisture.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine diced potatoes, chopped onion, minced garlic, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Drizzle olive oil over the potato mixture and toss to coat.
Add vanilla bean paste to the potato mixture and stir to combine.
Cut a large piece of parchment paper into a square and place the potato mixture in the center.
Fold the parchment paper over the potato mixture to create a papillote, sealing the edges by folding and pressing.
Place the papillote on a baking sheet and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the pilaf from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
